nodes.task:
INVESTIGATION ONLY — do NOT change any code, do NOT add any tests. Triage which SEO audit findings are already addressed by recent main-branch commits and which still require code changes.

Inputs to read:
- $FLT_RUN_DIR/seo-audit-2026-05-04.md — the 13-finding audit. Skip findings #5, #8, #10, #13 (out of scope).
- index.html (Vite root template; audit #1 says it contains duplicate <title>, <link rel="canonical">, meta description, og:* batches).
- scripts/prerender.js (audit #1 says this appends head tags rather than replacing them).
- server/seo.ts — already exports dedupeHead (line 21), buildSeoHtml (line 119), isCrawler (line 161), injectMetaIntoSpa (line 175). Determine whether scripts/prerender.js actually routes through these.
- server/routes/sitemap.ts (audit #2: sitemap URL form must agree with redirect target and canonical tag).
- server/index.ts handlers for /trade-ups (~line 781), /skins (~line 869), /trade-ups/:id (~line 295), /skins/:slug (~line 453), /collections/:slug (~line 332).
- public/robots.txt (audit #11 — does it Disallow /api/?).
- Recent commits: `git log --oneline -50` and `git show --stat dfb229d 6bd4411 26e9fc9` (PRs #95, #106, #107, #109 referenced in audit #10 as likely fixing #1 and #2).

For EACH of the in-scope findings below, classify status as "deploy-only" (already fixed in main, just needs production deploy) vs "code-needed" (still requires repo changes):
- #1 Duplicate canonical/title/description on content pages
- #2 Trailing-slash redirect ↔ canonical conflict (sitemap, prerender canonical, redirect target must agree)
- #3 Thin /trade-ups/{id} stubs (no H1, only first 3 of 10 inputs rendered, no structured content)
- #4 /skins/* 504 timeouts under crawl burst (no SSR cache)
- #9 Missing H1 on /trade-ups, /skins, /trade-ups/:id
- #11 Googlebot crawling /api/trade-ups/:id and 404-ing — find the source emitting these links (HTML, JSON-LD, sitemap, prerender)
- #12 Home <title> vs og:title disagreement

For each "code-needed" finding, list the specific files and line numbers the fix must touch (cite file:line). For "deploy-only", cite the merged commit/PR.

Write output to $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/triage.md with three sections:
1. ## Deploy-only — bulleted list keyed by finding number; one line each citing the merged commit/PR.
2. ## Code-needed — bulleted list keyed by finding number; for each, list file paths (file:line where useful) the fix must touch.
3. ## Deploy note — one paragraph confirming production VPS is on the stale flt/api-bench branch (per audit #10) and listing which merged-to-main commits need to roll out for the deploy-only items to take effect on the live site. The reconciler will copy this verbatim into the PR description.

Write a coder.md handoff next to your other artifacts summarizing what you found and any surprises (e.g. if the audit's premise is wrong about a specific finding).

flt workflow pass when triage.md and coder.md are written.

Fix duplicate <title>, <link rel="canonical">, meta-description, og:*, and twitter:* tags on prerendered content pages (audit finding #1) AND align the home <title> with og:title (audit finding #12).

Pre-step: read $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/triage.md. If BOTH #1 and #12 are listed under deploy-only, write a coder.md noting "no code changes required, fixes already on main; deploy from main per audit #10" and signal pass without modifying any code. If only one of #1/#12 is code-needed, do only that part.

Otherwise (TDD — write failing tests first, watch them fail, then code):

1. Write a failing unit test at tests/unit/seo-head-dedupe.test.ts (vitest — never jest). The test imports the prerender pipeline (or, if not exported, runs scripts/prerender.js end-to-end against a small fixture and reads the resulting HTML strings) and asserts:
   - For a sample prerendered blog post: EXACTLY one <title>, one <link rel="canonical">, one <meta name="description">, one og:title, one og:url, one twitter:* of each kind.
   - For the prerendered home (`/`): same one-of-each invariant.
   - For the home: <title> textContent equals og:title content (audit #12).
   Run `npx vitest run tests/unit/seo-head-dedupe.test.ts` — confirm FAIL.

2. Edit index.html (Vite root template) so the head contains AT MOST ONE of each meta/link tag class above. Audit shows the current template carries multiple duplicate batches that survive the build into dist/index.html.

3. Edit scripts/prerender.js so per-route output does NOT append duplicate head tags. The clean approach: route per-route head insertion through server/seo.ts's existing dedupeHead (server/seo.ts:21) or injectMetaIntoSpa (server/seo.ts:175) helpers. Replace any string-concat append logic with replace-or-insert semantics keyed on tag identity. Do not invent a parallel helper.

4. For #12, align home og:title with <title>. Update whichever side is wrong so the strings match exactly. If unsure, prefer the existing <title> "TradeUpBot — Find Profitable CS2 Trade-Ups from Real Listings" and update og:title to match.

5. Re-run `npx vitest run tests/unit/seo-head-dedupe.test.ts` — confirm green.
6. Run `bun run build` — must succeed. Spot-check that dist/index.html and at least one dist/blog/{slug}/index.html contain exactly one of each head tag.
7. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` — must be clean.

Do not touch sitemap, route handlers, or unrelated SEO emission. Write coder.md describing changes and any deviations.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Make the trailing-slash convention agree across (a) the 301 redirect target, (b) the prerendered canonical tag, and (c) sitemap URLs (audit finding #2). Per audit Fix B option 1: keep the redirect-to-slash and emit canonical-with-slash to match.

Pre-step: read $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/triage.md. If #2 is in deploy-only, write coder.md noting "no code changes required, fix already on main per PRs #95/#106; deploy from main" and signal pass without modifying code.

Otherwise (TDD):

1. Write a failing unit test at tests/unit/seo-trailing-slash.test.ts asserting:
   - server/routes/sitemap.ts emits all blog/feature/calculator/pricing/faq URLs WITH trailing slash (e.g. https://tradeupbot.app/blog/foo/, https://tradeupbot.app/faq/).
   - The prerender canonical output for each prerendered route (blog post, /faq, /pricing, /calculator, /features) has a trailing slash.
   - SSR-only routes (/trade-ups, /skins/X, /collections/X) remain WITHOUT trailing slash — audit #2 confirms those don't 301.
   Run `npx vitest run tests/unit/seo-trailing-slash.test.ts` — confirm FAIL.

2. Edit server/routes/sitemap.ts so prerendered/static routes emit the slash form. Leave SSR-only routes unchanged.

3. Edit scripts/prerender.js (and/or server/seo.ts buildSeoHtml at server/seo.ts:144 if that's the canonical emission point) so canonical href for prerendered routes uses the slash form. If head-dedupe consolidated head injection into a single helper, modify only the canonical-href construction there.

4. Re-run `npx vitest run tests/unit/seo-trailing-slash.test.ts` — confirm green.
5. `bun run build` — spot-check dist/blog/{slug}/index.html canonical equals https://tradeupbot.app/blog/{slug}/ with trailing slash.
6. `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean.

Do NOT touch nginx config (out of repo scope) or unrelated SSR routes. Write coder.md.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Expand /trade-ups/:id SSR from a 34-word stub into indexable content (audit #3), add a single H1 to the page (audit #9, detail-page portion), fix the input-slicing bug that renders only first 3 of 10 inputs, and remove any links Googlebot can follow to /api/trade-ups/:id (audit #11). Pre-step: read $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/triage.md. Skip any of #3/#9-detail/#11 that are listed as deploy-only. TDD: 1. Write a failing test at tests/unit/seo-trade-up-detail.test.ts (use tests/helpers/fixtures.ts makeTradeUp / makeListings to build a 10-input fixture; if rendering requires DB, place at tests/integration/seo-trade-up-detail.test.ts using the tradeupbot_test setup at tests/integration/setup.ts). Refactor the SSR rendering in server/index.ts /trade-ups/:id handler (~line 295) into a pure-ish renderTradeUpDetail(tradeUp, related) helper that returns the HTML string — the test imports that helper. Asserts: - exactly one

in rendered
; H1 text contains the profit dollar amount and ROI percent - all 10 input listings appear (count distinct rows / skin+condition+listing-id occurrences — must equal 10, NOT 3) - at least three

headings (e.g. Inputs, Outputs, Mechanics, Related Contracts) - the rendered HTML contains zero occurrences of "/api/trade-up" (covers /api/trade-ups/:id and /api/trade-up/:id) — search both attrs and JSON-LD blocks - a related-contracts list with at least one anchor to another /trade-ups/{id} or /collections/{slug} Run vitest — confirm FAIL. 2. In server/index.ts /trade-ups/:id handler (~line 295), locate the slice/loop that limits inputs to 3 and fix it to render all 10. The audit's evidence ("AWP | Queen's Gambit, AWP | Queen's Gambit, AWP | Queen's Gambit") strongly suggests an `.slice(0,3)` or similar. Render distinct input rows (skin name + condition + adjusted float + price). 3. Expand the rendered
into structured sections: -

{Type} Trade-Up — ${profit} Profit ({roi}% ROI)

-

Inputs

with all 10 inputs (table or list — skin, condition, float, price) -

Outputs

listing each output skin with probability and condition -

Mechanics

short paragraph (~3-5 sentences) explaining float averaging and output rolling -

Related Contracts

with 3-5 links to other trade-ups in the same collection or input rarity. Reuse an existing query in server/routes/trade-ups.ts as a model — DO NOT add new tables, indexes, or migrations. Target ~600 words of content per page. 4. Sweep these files for `/api/trade-ups/` and `/api/trade-up/` substrings emitted into HTML/JSON output: server/seo.ts, server/index.ts, server/routes/sitemap.ts, scripts/prerender.js, and any JSON-LD blocks in the trade-up renderer. Replace with the user-facing /trade-ups/:id path, or remove the link. 5. Re-run vitest — green. `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean. `bun run build` — succeeds. Do NOT modify other route handlers in server/index.ts (keep diff scoped to the /trade-ups/:id handler and any new pure helper you extract for testability). Write coder.md. flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add an in-process LRU cache to the Express SSR handlers for /skins/:slug and /collections/:slug to eliminate 504 timeouts under Googlebot crawl bursts (audit #4).

Pre-step: read $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/triage.md. If #4 is deploy-only, signal pass with a note. Else proceed.

TDD:

1. Write tests/integration/ssr-cache.test.ts (uses tradeupbot_test DB per tests/integration/setup.ts) that:
   - boots the Express app via the existing test harness
   - issues GET /skins/{seeded-skin-slug} twice in succession with `User-Agent: Googlebot`
   - asserts second response body equals the first (no recompute drift)
   - asserts the second request was a cache hit by reading hit/miss counters exposed from the new cache module
   - asserts TTL: after `vi.useFakeTimers(); vi.advanceTimersByTime(301_000)`, the next request is a miss
   - repeats the cycle for /collections/{seeded-collection-slug}
   Run `npx vitest run tests/integration/ssr-cache.test.ts` — confirm FAIL.

2. Create server/ssr-cache.ts exporting `cachedSsr(key: string, ttlMs: number, builder: () => Promise<string>): Promise<string>` plus `getStats(): { hits: number; misses: number }` and `resetStats(): void`. Implementation: a small Map-based LRU with TTL — ~30-50 LOC, NO external dependency unless `lru-cache` is already in package.json (check first with `grep '"lru-cache"' package.json`). Do not add a new Redis-backed layer; this is per-process only.

3. In server/index.ts wrap ONLY the response composers for `app.get("/skins/:slug", ...)` (~line 453) and `app.get("/collections/:slug", ...)` (~line 332) so that when server/seo.ts isCrawler(req.headers['user-agent']) returns true, the response body is built via cachedSsr. Cache key: `"skins:" + slug + ":" + stOrSouvenirFlags` (and the analog for collections). TTL: 5 minutes (300_000 ms). Non-crawler requests bypass the cache.

4. Re-run vitest — green. `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean. `bun run build` — succeeds.

Do NOT modify other route handlers. Do NOT change isCrawler logic in server/seo.ts. Write coder.md.

flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.

Add exactly one

to the SSR output of the /trade-ups and /skins listing pages (audit #9). The /trade-ups/:id detail-page H1 is handled by the thin-detail-pages node — do NOT touch /trade-ups/:id here. Pre-step: read $FLT_RUN_DIR/artifacts/triage.md. If #9 is deploy-only, signal pass with note. Else proceed. TDD: 1. Write tests/unit/seo-listing-h1.test.ts. Refactor minimally if needed to expose a pure renderer for each listing page (e.g. renderTradeUpsList(items) and renderSkinsList(items)) so the test can call it without booting Express. For each of /trade-ups and /skins SSR responses, assert: - exactly one

in the rendered HTML - /trade-ups H1 text matches /Profitable CS2 Trade-Up Contracts/i - /skins H1 text matches /CS2 Skin Database/i - any pre-existing

on /trade-ups ("Find Profitable CS2 Trade-Up Contracts") is preserved as

only if you keep it as a subheading; otherwise it's promoted to the

. The test should not duplicate-assert headings. Run vitest — confirm FAIL. 2. Edit server/index.ts /trade-ups handler (~line 781). Either (a) promote the existing

Find Profitable CS2 Trade-Up Contracts

to

Profitable CS2 Trade-Up Contracts

, OR (b) prepend

Profitable CS2 Trade-Up Contracts

and convert the existing

to a subheading — pick whichever yields the cleanest single-h1 result. Do not introduce a second h1. 3. Edit server/index.ts /skins handler (~line 869). Insert

CS2 Skin Database — Prices, Floats, Trade-Ups

at the top of
. 4. Re-run vitest — green. `npx tsc --noEmit` — clean. `bun run build` — succeeds. Do NOT touch other route handlers (especially not /trade-ups/:id, /skins/:slug, /collections/:slug). Write coder.md. flt workflow pass when tests are green and your coder.md handoff is written.
